title: Day
content:
The Sun arises in the East,
    Cloth'd in robes of blood and gold;
    Swords and spears and wrath increast
    All around his bosom roll'd
    Crown'd with warlike fires and raging desires.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Blake, titled "Day", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
